meta.note: subscription auth; codex reports tokens, not USD

A.missing: Unexpectedness is weakened to not knowing the event would happen; an anticipated but uncertain appearance also satisfies this.
A.reason: It captures an event changing something from not visible to visible, but lack of knowledge does not criterially distinguish an unexpected appearance.

B.missing: It omits unexpectedness and conflates the appearing event with the entity that is seen.
B.reason: The generic event merely changes from unseen to seen and could describe many observable events.

C.missing: It replaces not anticipating the appearance with merely not thinking about the entity.
C.reason: The visibility transition is present, but not thinking about something does not render its unexpected appearance.

D.missing: The unexpectedness of becoming visible is entirely absent.
D.reason: It gives only a generic transition from inability to ability to see something.

E.reason: It sharply renders an event in which something becomes visible to an observer who had not thought this would occur.
